jokes.text: What, do you think there's two lines and we're in it and you're the only genius that got in the other line?
jokes.setup: Man tries to cut in line claiming there are two lines
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically points out the absurd logic of his excuse
jokes.explanation: Strong character-establishing moment showing Liz's justice-focused personality through relatable situation comedy

jokes.text: I'm buying all the hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Liz's nuclear response to buy all hot dogs
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that establishes Liz's principled but impractical nature - becomes a running reference

jokes.text: That's Pam, The Overly-Confident Morbidly Obese Woman
jokes.punchline: Brutal, matter-of-fact description of the character
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the show's dark comedy tone - the casual cruelty of TV behind-the-scenes

jokes.text: This fat suit smells like corn chips
jokes.setup: Actress in fat suit for sketch
jokes.punchline: Specific, unsexy detail about the costume
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's vanity through unglamorous detail

jokes.text: Apparently, you can't say he's got a vagina
jokes.setup: Standards and Practices complaint about Michael Jackson bit
jokes.punchline: The specific, absurd content restriction
jokes.explanation: Great meta-comedy about TV censorship and the absurdity of content standards

jokes.text: Well, I just spent 150 bucks on wieners
jokes.punchline: Callback to hot dog purchase with juvenile double entendre
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that reinforces Liz's character while adding wordplay

jokes.text: Are there other black nerds, or is it just you and Urkel?
jokes.setup: Toofer explains what a samovar is
jokes.punchline: Cerie's casually offensive but innocent question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cerie character moment - politically incorrect but delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: Have you ever noticed when you eat a lot of cheese... How is that different from what I was doing?
jokes.setup: Josh attempts Jay Leno impression multiple times
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to see the difference between his attempts
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Josh's complete lack of self-awareness about his terrible impression

jokes.text: Where's Gary? Gary's dead.
jokes.setup: Liz asks about their boss Gary
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact delivery of shocking news
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - casual delivery makes the shocking revelation hilarious and memorable

jokes.text: I'm Jack Donaghy, new VP of Development for NBC-GE-Universal-Kmart
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd corporate merger names
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jack's corporate absurdity and the show's satirical take on media conglomerates

jokes.text: We own Kmart now? / No.
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual 'No' deflates the corporate grandstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation - builds up corporate complexity then casually admits it's nonsense

jokes.text: 5 inches, but it's thick
jokes.punchline: Sexually suggestive comment taken out of context
jokes.explanation: Simple innuendo humor that works for character establishment but not particularly clever

jokes.text: New York third-wave feminist, college-educated, single and pretending to be happy about it, overscheduled, undersexed
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate psychological profile delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character establishment - shows Jack's analytical nature while perfectly capturing Liz

jokes.text: That is dead-on! What, are you gonna guess my weight now?
jokes.setup: Liz amazed by Jack's accurate assessment
jokes.punchline: Her immediate defensive follow-up about weight
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's insecurity and defensive humor

jokes.text: The GE trivection oven cooks perfect food five times faster than a conventional oven because it uses three kinds of heat
jokes.punchline: Jack launches into full product pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate satire - Jack literally selling appliances in creative meeting

jokes.text: With three kinds of heat, you can cook a turkey in 22 minutes
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd cooking time claim
jokes.explanation: Escalates the product pitch absurdity with specific ridiculous claim

jokes.text: I'm the new Vice President of east-coast television and microwave-oven programming.
jokes.punchline: Job title that combines TV and appliances in bureaucratic language
jokes.explanation: Great corporate absurdity - title that makes no sense but sounds official

jokes.text: You have the boldness of a much-younger woman
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about her age
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack-style insult disguised as compliment, establishing his character

jokes.text: When I played that lady rapist on Law & Order, guess what the makeup guy put on under my eyes to take the puffiness out. Hemorrhoid cream.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about gross beauty trick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - vanity combined with inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: Hi. Greta Johanssen... short for Lugreta.
jokes.punchline: Made-up name explanation that makes no linguistic sense
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - completely made-up nickname etymology delivered seriously

jokes.text: I used to have two cats. But then I moved to this place with hardwood floors, so we had to put them down. I'm joking.
jokes.setup: Cat wrangler talking about loving cats
jokes.punchline: Shockingly dark punchline followed by weak save
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy misdirection - the 'I'm joking' makes it even funnier

jokes.text: You're missing that third kind of heat.
jokes.punchline: Jack applying oven metaphor to TV programming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that perfectly captures Jack's corporate mindset - applying appliance logic to creativity

jokes.text: Okay, the black guy? / The black movie star.
jokes.punchline: Jack's correction showing his corporate euphemistic language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's corporate speak and Liz's directness

jokes.text: I am a Jedi!
jokes.punchline: Random outburst suggesting mental instability
jokes.explanation: Establishes Tracy's unpredictable nature, though somewhat predictable 'crazy' humor

jokes.text: It's not illegal to fall asleep on your neighbor's roof. / Nor should it be.
jokes.punchline: Pete's philosophical agreement with absurd legal position
jokes.explanation: Good absurd legal argument with Pete's unexpected philosophical support

jokes.text: Sometimes, when I have these stress dreams, if I go to sleep in the dream, I come out of it. This is not a dream.
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre logic for dealing with stress
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist character moment showing Liz's stress-induced weird logic

jokes.text: 'This is not a dream.'
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her dream escape strategy
jokes.punchline: Pete's matter-of-fact reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete response - supportive but realistic, delivered with care

jokes.text: I'm known for being reasonable.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if he'll back off if meeting goes badly
jokes.punchline: Jack's self-assessment that contradicts everything we've seen
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic character line - Jack's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: You're dressed for Burger King. Should we make it Burger King?
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's not dressed for fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Jack's cutting insult about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - economical, cutting, and character-specific insult

jokes.text: Yeah, if I was president of the Philippines
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting how she should dress
jokes.punchline: Liz's specific, random geographic reference
jokes.explanation: Good Liz comeback with oddly specific reference showing her quick wit

jokes.text: Did he just say the word 'pumpkin' to me?
jokes.punchline: Tracy's inexplicable offense at the word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Tracy's completely irrational reaction to innocent word

jokes.text: I'm buggin'. I can't do this. I cannot eat here. I got to go someplace where they make food that I like.
jokes.punchline: Complete restaurant rejection over minor perceived slight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tracy's impulsive, dramatic personality

jokes.text: Ooh, ooh, goodness gracious! You hooligans better watch out! 'Cause this honky grandma be trippin'!
jokes.setup: Josh trying to remember Tracy movie
jokes.punchline: Offensive racial impression delivered innocently
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy showing Josh's tone-deafness and casual racism

jokes.text: I had, like, four hot dogs this morning.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to the morning's hot dog purchase
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing the consequence of Liz's morning decision

jokes.text: I had, like, four hot dogs this morning
jokes.punchline: Liz referencing her earlier hot dog purchase
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces the opening sequence and Liz's character

jokes.text: That's racist. I'm not on crack. I'm straight-up mentally ill.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his anger about US Weekly
jokes.punchline: Correction that somehow makes the situation worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Tracy's 'defense' that's actually more damaging than the original accusation

jokes.text: I'm not on crack. I'm straight-up mentally ill.
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact correction about his mental health
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - takes expected celebrity denial and makes it worse in unexpected way

jokes.text: It's for my face
jokes.punchline: Jenna's defensive clarification
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jenna's vanity and embarrassment

jokes.text: Affirmative action was designed to keep women and minorities in competition with each other, to distract us while white dudes inject AIDS into our chicken nuggets
jokes.setup: Tracy's conspiracy theory about white dudes
jokes.punchline: Completely insane escalation to AIDS chicken nuggets
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation from reasonable-sounding conspiracy to complete insanity

jokes.text: That's a metaphor. / Sure.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's clarification that somehow makes it worse + Liz's defeated agreement
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up - Tracy thinking 'metaphor' makes it reasonable + Liz's exhausted acceptance

jokes.text: Your teeth
jokes.setup: Liz asking how he knows she's not rich
jokes.punchline: Tracy's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and brutal honesty - single word that devastates Liz

jokes.text: I don't have sex for a living. Neither do we. Neither do they. Yes, they do.
jokes.punchline: Escalating confusion about strippers' actual job
jokes.explanation: Great escalating confusion with perfect timing and Liz's naivety

jokes.text: He's got charisma. No, that's Charisma over there.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's literal interpretation as stripper's name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with perfect setup/payoff

jokes.text: I wish you were here.
jokes.setup: Liz in strip club calling Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment to innocent page while in compromising location
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing Liz's social awkwardness in inappropriate setting

jokes.text: Pete got fired this morning. That's so weird that you don't know that.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent delivery of shocking news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - delivers devastating news with complete innocence

jokes.text: From Chicago? / I was in Scottie Pippin's wedding.
jokes.setup: Jenna surprised Liz moved from Chicago for show
jokes.punchline: Liz's random celebrity name-drop that doesn't answer the question
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's awkward bragging in inappropriate context

jokes.text: I was in Scottie Pippin's wedding
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity name-drop as proof of success
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's desperate attempts at name-dropping

jokes.text: No, you're not listening. You're not a good listener.
jokes.setup: Jenna misunderstanding who to be mad at
jokes.punchline: Liz's frustrated assessment of Jenna's listening skills in crisis moment
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic - Liz's exasperation with Jenna's self-centeredness

jokes.text: This eye doesn't open all the way because when I was little, my sister peed in it
jokes.punchline: Disturbing childhood trauma as explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing overshare that's perfectly in character for Jenna

jokes.text: Peanut's not an actor. I just rescued her from a lab
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about lab animal
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy delivered casually, consistent with character's earlier cat joke

jokes.text: Me and two other dudes and a girl with messed-up fingers
jokes.punchline: Specific disturbing detail about foster sister
jokes.explanation: Dark character development with specific unsettling detail

jokes.text: Are you peeing?
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Tracy and Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz's horrified realization about Tracy's behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing to ruin touching moment with crude reality

jokes.text: Don't buy all the hot dogs, okay?
jokes.setup: Pete giving life advice to Liz
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to morning incident as metaphor for life choices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that elevates the hot dog incident to metaphor for Liz's impulsive decision-making

jokes.text: We're two minutes short!
jokes.punchline: Desperate scrambling as show falls apart
jokes.explanation: Standard TV production chaos comedy, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: This honky grandma be trippin'!
jokes.punchline: Reference to Josh's earlier impression
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tracy's familiarity with his own work

jokes.text: I am the third heat!
jokes.punchline: Tracy embracing Jack's oven metaphor for his role on the show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant culmination - Tracy claiming Jack's corporate metaphor as his identity, perfect character moment

jokes.text: I am the third heat!
jokes.punchline: Reference to Jack's oven metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that ties Tracy's arrival to Jack's corporate metaphor

jokes.text: You weigh 127 pounds. / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming he doesn't know everything about Liz
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately proving he knows her exact weight + Liz's defeated acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier weight-guessing threat, Liz's resigned 'Yeah' is perfect

jokes.text: You weigh 127 pounds
jokes.setup: Jack claiming he doesn't know everything about Liz
jokes.punchline: Immediate proof that he does know everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reinforces Jack's analytical nature and proves Liz wrong

jokes.text: You will be hearing from Peanut's lawyer
jokes.punchline: Treating cat like celebrity with legal representation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of animal actor premise with absurdist legal threat

jokes.text: Hey, you will be hearing from Peanut's lawyer.
jokes.setup: Cat attack incident on live TV
jokes.punchline: Threat of legal action on behalf of traumatized rescue cat
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation - legal representation for a cat, shows character priorities

jokes.text: I can't wait to do this with you every week.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's enthusiasm for what was clearly a disaster for everyone else
jokes.explanation: Good character ending - Tracy's obliviousness to the chaos he's created
